mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.07% (near zero lower bound) combined with CPI at 234.582 (stable) signals monetary accommodation. Sustained low rates compress long-term discount rates, causing 10-year Treasury yields to decline as investors rotate into duration. This mechanism operates continuously in low-rate regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.07% indicates near-zero policy rate
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises more than 15 basis points over 20 trading days despite FEDFUNDS remaining below 0.15%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 6.6% represents labor market slack. If UNRATE rises above 7.0% within the next 60 days, it signals recession risk, triggering flight-to-safety demand for long-duration Treasuries and causing yields to fall sharply. This threshold-triggered mechanism activates only when unemployment crosses the 7.0% barrier.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 7.0% within 60 trading days
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.6% is elevated relative to 2018 levels
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with rising unemployment risk
failure_condition: UNRATE crosses 7.0% but 10-year Treasury yield rises more than 10 basis points over the subsequent 20 trading days, or trigger fires in fewer than 5% of 60-day windows where UNRATE > 7.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) at 427,829M and housing starts (HOUST) at 999K show weakening demand momentum. When both series decline simultaneously by more than 2% month-over-month, it signals demand contraction that reduces inflation expectations and causes 10-year Treasury yields to fall. This interaction-driven mechanism requires joint deterioration in both demand proxies.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline >2% MoM within 30 days
regime_hint: demand-side slowdown with synchronized weakness across consumption and housing
failure_condition: Both RSAFS and HOUST decline >2% MoM but 10-year Treasury yield rises more than 12 basis points over next 20 trading days, or joint trigger occurs in fewer than 3% of rolling 30-day windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 101.0251 is near trend. A sustained decline in INDPRO below 100.5 over 2 consecutive months signals supply-side weakness and stagflation risk. In this regime, investors demand higher yields on long-duration assets to compensate for inflation uncertainty, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 100.5 for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: INDPRO remains below 100.5 for 2+ months but 10-year Treasury yield falls more than 15 basis points over subsequent 20 trading days, or trigger fires in fewer than 8% of 60-day windows.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 137,499K and unemployment (UNRATE) at 6.6% show labor market softening. When PAYEMS growth falls below 50K MoM while UNRATE rises simultaneously, it signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumption expectations. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts 10-year Treasury yields will fall as growth expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth <50K MoM AND UNRATE rises >0.2pp within 30 days
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS growth has slowed in recent months
regime_hint: labor market deterioration with synchronized employment and unemployment weakness
failure_condition: Both conditions trigger but 10-year Treasury yield rises more than 10 basis points over next 20 trading days, or joint trigger occurs in fewer than 4% of rolling 30-day windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) at 15,965.6B (Q2 2019) combined with RSAFS at 427,829M indicates slowing demand growth. A quarter-over-quarter GDP growth rate below 1.5% signals late-cycle deceleration. In this regime, the Fed cuts rates, and 10-year Treasury yields fall as the yield curve flattens and long-duration assets become attractive.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ growth falls below 1.5% annualized rate
failure_condition: GDPC1 QoQ growth falls below 1.5% but 10-year Treasury yield rises more than 20 basis points over subsequent 20 trading days, or trigger fires in fewer than 6% of rolling 60-day windows.

mechanism_narrative: CPI at 234.582 (stable) and FEDFUNDS at 0.07% create a negative real rate environment. If CPI accelerates above 2.5% YoY while FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%, the Fed faces policy constraints. Investors anticipate future rate hikes to combat inflation, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise in anticipation of monetary tightening.
candidate_trigger: CPI YoY growth exceeds 2.5% while FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%
evidence_refs: fact:Real rates are negative at current policy levels
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with rising inflation pressure
failure_condition: CPI YoY exceeds 2.5% and FEDFUNDS < 1.0% but 10-year Treasury yield falls more than 8 basis points over next 5 trading days, or trigger occurs in fewer than 5% of rolling 5-day windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 999K are near cycle lows. A sustained decline below 950K for 2 consecutive months signals housing market collapse, reducing construction employment and consumer wealth. This regime-specific mechanism predicts 10-year Treasury yields will fall sharply as recession risk rises and flight-to-safety demand intensifies.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 950K for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: housing market contraction with recession risk
failure_condition: HOUST remains below 950K for 2+ months but 10-year Treasury yield rises more than 12 basis points over subsequent 20 trading days, or trigger fires in fewer than 7% of rolling 60-day windows.
